title: Melty Cheesy Japanese Pork Gyoza
description: A Japanese Kansai dish featuring roasted pepper and rest meat, this recipe is a crowd pleaser with its nutty and cheesy flavor, and is also nut free friendly. It is a perfect dish for those who love Japanese cuisine and cheesy food.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, roasted red pepper, mozzarella cheese, soy sauce, sesame oil, green onion, and garlic, and mix well until everything is fully incorporated.
Lay a gyoza wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water and run it along the edge of the wrapper.
Fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point, and press the edges together to seal the gyoza.
Repeat the process with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Heat a large skillet with about an inch of water over medium high heat and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to medium low and add the gyoza to the skillet, being careful not to overcrowd it.
Steam the gyoza for about 5 minutes, then remove the lid and continue to cook until the bottom of the gyoza is golden brown.
Serve the gyoza hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
